name: cjk-agent-fixtures localization friction taxonomy
model: If a system assumes X, then failure Y occurs in context Z.
scripts_covered: Rare CJK Extension B kanji (above U+FFFF)

format.name: Format mismatch
format.assumes: one character is one byte, one UTF-16 unit, one display column, and one code point, in a single canonical encoding
format.failure: text is cut mid-character, the cursor lands in the wrong column, an astral character or a joined cluster is split, and two equal strings compare unequal
format.context: input is CJK (3 UTF-8 bytes, 2 columns), Hangul, RTL (2 bytes), carries combining marks (0 columns, NFC vs NFD), lives above U+FFFF (2 UTF-16 units), wears a width variant (NFKC), or is a multi-code-point grapheme (ZWJ / variation selector)

legal_schema.name: Legal / schema mismatch
legal_schema.assumes: one global shape for an address, an invoice, a tax line, or a receipt
legal_schema.failure: a required field has nowhere to go, or a value is rejected by a validator that does not know the local rule
legal_schema.context: Japanese invoice retention (インボイス / 適格請求書), the 特商法 disclosure block, postal and name-order shape, or a tax-identifier format the global schema never modeled
legal_schema.note: Not exercised by this input-handling artifact. The compliance surface lives in a separate validator layer; listed for completeness because the taxonomy spans all three layers.

cultural_ux.name: Cultural / UX mismatch
cultural_ux.assumes: every keystroke commits immediately, focus changes are harmless, the space key produces an ASCII space, and text flows left to right
cultural_ux.failure: the key that confirms an IME candidate is read as submit, pending composition is dropped on focus change, the field freezes after refocus, a fullwidth space survives a blank check, or a line lays out backwards
cultural_ux.context: a user typing through an IME (kana, Hangul, pinyin), whose space bar in fullwidth mode emits U+3000, or entering a right-to-left script (Arabic, Hebrew)

fixtures.breaks: An Enter handler that ignores isComposing submits on the key that confirms an IME candidate, eating the first attempt at every message.
fixtures.asserts: The confirm Enter does not submit; only the real Enter does.

fixtures.breaks: Slicing text by raw byte index lands inside a multi-byte sequence (CJK/Hangul are 3 bytes, Arabic/Hebrew are 2) and produces U+FFFD.
fixtures.asserts: A byte slice corrupts; a code-point slice keeps characters whole.

fixtures.breaks: A renderer that counts characters instead of columns misplaces the cursor: fullwidth CJK and Hangul take 2 columns, combining marks take 0.
fixtures.asserts: displayWidth counts columns; the naive per-character count is flagged wrong.

fixtures.breaks: Focus moves away mid-composition and compositionend never fires, so the pending text is silently dropped.
fixtures.asserts: A correct editor commits the pending composition on blur; a dropping one loses it.

fixtures.breaks: isComposing stays true after focus leaves during composition, so the field rejects every later keystroke and looks frozen.
fixtures.asserts: After blur and refocus, a plain keystroke is accepted again.

fixtures.breaks: A field that hard-codes LTR or guesses direction from the first character lays Arabic/Hebrew out backwards when the line starts with a digit or bracket (123 mrhba).
fixtures.asserts: Base direction follows the first strong character (Unicode Bidi P2/P3); leading neutrals are skipped.

fixtures.breaks: The same text precomposed (NFC) vs decomposed (NFD) compares unequal under raw ===, so a login fails, a file isn't found, or a dedup keeps both copies.
fixtures.asserts: A normalized compare treats the forms as equal; the raw compare is flagged wrong.

fixtures.breaks: A code point above U+FFFF (rare kanji like 𠮷 U+20BB7, every emoji) is two UTF-16 code units, so str.length over-counts and a slice at an odd unit boundary leaves a lone surrogate that decodes to U+FFFD. Distinct from #2: this crosses a UTF-16 unit boundary, not a UTF-8 byte one.
fixtures.asserts: A UTF-16-unit slice corrupts; a code-point slice keeps the astral character whole.

fixtures.breaks: Halfwidth katakana (ﾊﾝｶｸ) and fullwidth ASCII (Ａ１) are different code points from ハンカク and A1; raw === and even canonical (NFC, #7) comparison report them unequal, so search, dedup, and uniqueness checks miss them.
fixtures.asserts: A compatibility (NFKC) compare folds the width variants equal; the raw and NFC compares are flagged short.

fixtures.breaks: A ZWJ emoji family, a skin-tone emoji, a flag, or a Japanese ideographic variation sequence (a name kanji + U+E0100) is one grapheme over several code points; even the correct code-point slice from #2 and #8 splits it, stripping a skin tone, breaking a flag, or changing a name kanji's glyph.
fixtures.asserts: A grapheme slice keeps the cluster whole; a code-point slice is shown tearing it apart.

fixtures.breaks: The Japanese IME in fullwidth mode types U+3000 (ideographic space) on the space bar; an ASCII-only trim or blank check leaves it, so a required field of only 　　 passes the not-empty check and a value of 田中　 never matches 田中 on lookup or dedup.
fixtures.asserts: A Unicode-aware trim (native trim(), strings.TrimSpace) removes U+3000; the ASCII-only trim is flagged for leaving it.
